summary: chit already records one append-only audit receipt per run (audit/runs/<runId>/events.jsonl plus content-addressed blobs/) and a per-run, body-free reader (apps/cli/src/audit/reader.ts: summarizeRun, sumUsage, safeReadEvents). The only gap is cross-run roll-up. Recommend a single pure function aggregateReceipts(store, opts) that folds existing RunSummary values into one metrics-only aggregate, reusing reader.ts, never opening blobs, surfaced as a read-only CLI subcommand next to 'chit audit list'.

dataSources.name: audit-receipts
dataSources.format: append-only JSONL, one AuditEvent per line
dataSources.note: Closed discriminated union: run.started, step.started, adapter.call.started, adapter.event, adapter.call.completed, step.completed, step.failed, loop.iteration.recorded, run.completed. Every event has runId and ISO ts. This is the only source the v1 reader reads.
dataSources.bodies: <stateDir>/audit/runs/<runId>/blobs/<sha256> hold prompts/outputs; NEVER read by the reader

dataSources.name: loop-log
dataSources.format: append-only JSONL: header, iteration records, stop record
dataSources.note: Owns loop-stop status (converged/blocked/max-iterations/needs-decision/cancelled). A v1 non-goal to join; convergence verdicts are already in the audit loop.iteration.recorded event.

dataSources.name: jobs
dataSources.format: one JSON job record per run
dataSources.note: Durable worker state; carries auditRefs[]. Not needed for the v1 aggregate.

linkingKeys.runId: public id; on every AuditEvent, job record, and plan step
linkingKeys.loopId: internal loop-log key on loop jobs and loop headers
linkingKeys.scope: user-chosen label; potentially identifying, keep as filter input only
linkingKeys.auditRef: an iteration's audit run id; links loop iteration to its transcript

stateDir: $XDG_STATE_HOME/chit (falls back to ~/.local/state/chit); resolved per layer by defaultAuditDir()/loopStateDir()/defaultJobsDir()

algorithm: Enumerate runs via store.listRuns(); it returns ids in ARBITRARY filesystem order, not sorted.
Per run: safeReadEvents() then summarizeRun(); never set includeBodies; never open blobs/.
Sort the resulting summaries by startedAt descending (newest first) BEFORE applying limit, exactly as listAudit does in reader.ts; do not rely on listRuns order for limit.
An empty/corrupt log folds in as a counted skip, not a throw.
Fold each RunSummary plus the run's loop.iteration.recorded events into accumulators.

options.scope: filter to one scope (input only, never a grouped output dimension)
options.limit: cap runs kept after sorting summaries by startedAt descending; listRuns order is arbitrary, so sort before slicing

outputShape.usage: summed AdapterUsage: inputTokens, outputTokens, cachedInputTokens, reasoningTokens, totalTokens, estimatedCostUsd
outputShape.convergence: verdict/decision counts (proceed|revise|block) and total findingCount from loop.iteration.recorded

metrics.note: Aggregate-only corpus shape measured locally to size the reader; no bodies read. Values are approximate.
metrics.designImplication: adapter.event/blob content is ~200x the rest of the line count and must be skipped, not summarized; folding header+completion+loop-iteration per run is cheap. Token totals are large, so sums must be 64-bit-safe numbers and cost a single summed float.

implementationFiles.purpose: Pure aggregateReceipts fold over reader.ts; defines ReceiptAggregate and AggregateOptions.
Wire a new read-only subcommand that calls aggregateReceipts and prints a table, with a --json flag for the structured object.
Source of summarizeRun, sumUsage, safeReadEvents, RunSummary; do not duplicate this logic.

cases: mixed surfaces and statuses counted correctly
usage summed across runs (tokens and cost)
a corrupt or empty events.jsonl is counted as skipped, not thrown
invariant: no blob/body is ever read (includeBodies never set)

risks: adapter.event rows dominate line counts (~200x); reading them per run is fine but never aggregate or resolve their blobs.
Some runs are incomplete (run.started without run.completed: ~809 vs ~804 locally); treat incomplete as its own status, do not drop them.
Token totals reach hundreds of millions; keep numeric sums 64-bit-safe and cost a single float, no per-call arrays in output.
scope is a user-chosen label and may identify work; keep it an input filter, never a grouped output dimension in v1.
Corpus is local and single-user; counts here size the reader, they are not a product claim.
privacyRules: Never read audit/runs/<runId>/blobs/; never set includeBodies. inputBlob/outputBlob/rawBlob address prompts, model outputs, and raw adapter streams and must never be touched.
Emit only metrics and low-cardinality safe enums (surface, status, loop verdict) plus numeric sums.
Never emit cwd, commandArgs, manifestPath, session refs, or step.failed error strings: they can carry absolute paths or private data.
Tolerate corruption: a bad log is a counted skip, not a failure of the whole aggregate.
These same rules governed this handoff: no prompts, model outputs, absolute local paths, customer names, internal links, raw receipt bodies, or secret-like values are included.
